mnemonics.title: AI Types Memory Aid
mnemonics.content: Remember: N-A-S = Narrow (specific task) → AGI (General/Human-like) → ASI (Super/超越人类)

mnemonics.title: AI History Timeline
mnemonics.content: 1950: Turing Test → 1956: AI Coined → 1966: ELIZA → 1997: Deep Blue → 2012: Deep Learning Breakthrough → 2022: ChatGPT

mnemonics.title: AI vs ML vs DL
mnemonics.content: AI is the BRAIN, ML is the WAY TO LEARN, DL is DEEP LEARNING (Brain's neural networks)

acronyms.usage: The broader field of creating intelligent machines
AI subset where systems learn from data
ML subset using deep neural networks
Human-level AI capable of any cognitive task
AI designed for specific tasks (current AI)
Symbolic AI using logic and rules
AI ability to understand human language

visualTips.visualization: Draw three concentric circles: Outer=AI, Middle=ML (inside AI), Inner=DL (inside ML). Label each with examples.
Think of a staircase: Step 1 (Narrow/specific tasks), Step 2 (General/human-like), Step 3 (Super/beyond human).
Left brain (logic/rules) = Symbolic; Right brain (neural patterns) = Connectionist; Show them as two different problem-solving paths.
Draw three rooms: Room A (Human), Room B (Machine), Room C (Evaluator). Evaluator asks questions via keyboard and must identify which is human.

analogies.analogy: AI is like a large umbrella covering all attempts to make machines intelligent, under which ML and DL are smaller umbrellas.
ML is like teaching a child by showing examples rather than giving a rulebook - learning from experience/data.
Deep Learning is like how our brain processes images through layers of neurons, recognizing edges first, then shapes, then complex objects.
ANI is like a calculator (great at math, nothing else), while AGI would be like a person who can do math, write poetry, and fix cars.
